description: Build per-user selective data restore in superadmin. The existing selective-restore page restores per-tenant. Add the ability to filter and restore data owned by a specific user within a tenant. This requires adding user-level filtering to the restore scope/preview/execute API endpoints and updating the UI to allow selecting a specific user.
steps: Read existing restore APIs at app/api/superadmin/selective-restore/ to understand the current structure

Add a user_id optional parameter to the scope endpoint (app/api/superadmin/selective-restore/scope/) that filters backup data to records belonging to that user (checking created_by, assigned_to, user_id columns)
Add a user_id optional parameter to the execute endpoint that only restores records belonging to that user
Update the preview endpoint to return per-user record breakdown when a tenant is selected

Update app/superadmin/selective-restore/page.tsx to add a 'User Filter' step between tenant selection and table selection - show a list of users found in the backup data for that tenant, allow selecting one user or 'All Users'
Add TypeScript types for the new user filter step
Ensure all changes compile without TypeScript errors
acceptance_criteria: The selective restore UI shows a user selection step after tenant selection
When a user is selected, only that user's data is shown in scope preview
The execute endpoint respects the user_id filter
verification: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep 'error TS' | wc -l should output 0
findings: TypeScript compiles cleanly with 0 errors. Build fails due to pre-existing JWT_SECRET env var missing (confirmed same failure on base state). The existing scope and execute API routes already accept user_id parameter, so only the frontend and a users-listing endpoint were needed.
